data.title: Polymarket Account Places $100,000 Bet on Denver Nuggets in Game 6
data.description: A Polymarket account, which previously incurred a loss of approximately $1.5 million, has placed a $100,000 wager on the Denver Nuggets to defeat the Minnesota Timberwolves in the Western Conference first-round Game 6. According to NS3.AI, this position was established roughly 50 minutes before the game started, with an average price of 73 cents.

data.title: NBA Playoffs: Timberwolves vs. Nuggets Game 6 Preview
data.description:
The NBA playoffs continue with the Western Conference first-round Game 6 between the Minnesota Timberwolves and the Denver Nuggets. According to Odaily, a notable account with a loss of approximately $1.5 million recently invested $100,000 in favor of the Nuggets defeating the Timberwolves, with an opening price of 73¢.

The game is scheduled to start at 9:30 a.m. UTC+8. Currently, the Timberwolves lead the series 3-2 against the Nuggets. However, the Timberwolves face challenges as their star player, Anthony Edwards, is sidelined due to a knee injury. Additionally, Ayo Dosunmu will miss the game due to a calf injury, and Donte DiVincenzo is out for the season with a torn right Achilles tendon.

On the Nuggets' side, Aaron Gordon will not play due to left calf muscle tension, and Peyton Watson is also out with a right hamstring strain.

data.title: Australia's First Quarter PPI Shows Moderate Growth
data.description: Australia's Producer Price Index (PPI) for the first quarter recorded a quarterly growth rate of 0.4%, according to Jin10. This marks a decrease from the previous quarter's rate of 0.80%, indicating a moderation in producer price growth.

data.title: AI Investment Boom Influences Global Trade Amid Energy Shock
data.description: Recent data highlights the contrasting impacts of the AI investment surge and the ongoing energy shock due to the Iran conflict. Bloomberg posted on X, noting that while AI-driven advancements are boosting trade, investment, and consumption in certain sectors, the energy crisis stemming from the Iran war is causing disruptions in others. The dual forces are shaping economic activities differently across regions, with AI innovations fostering growth and the energy shock posing challenges to stability.

data.title: Google Q1 2026 Earnings Boost Alphabet by $300B; Meta Falls $175B
data.description: Google's Q1 2026 earnings have propelled Alphabet's market value by $300 billion in a single session, while Meta experienced a $175 billion drop, both influenced by the same AI capex narrative. According to BeInCrypto, the contrasting market reactions highlight investor sentiment towards AI investments, with Alphabet benefiting from positive earnings results and Meta facing challenges. The financial impact underscores the significance of strategic AI spending in shaping market valuations for tech giants.

data.title: XRP Spot ETF Experiences Significant Outflow
data.description: XRP spot ETF saw a notable outflow of $5.83 million on April 30, according to ChainCatcher. The Bitwise XRP ETF was the only fund to experience this outflow, contributing to a historical total net inflow of $422 million. As of the latest report, the total net asset value of the XRP spot ETF stands at $1.04 billion, with an XRP net asset ratio of 1.23%. The cumulative historical net inflow has reached $1.29 billion.

data.title: Arbitrum Freezes $72 Million Linked to North Korean Hackers Using Emergency Powers
data.description: Arbitrum has exercised emergency powers to freeze $72 million associated with North Korean hackers. According to NS3.AI, this action marks the first use of a 9-of-12 multi-signature emergency execution, highlighting the severity of the threat to decentralized finance (DeFi). Security Council member Griff Green, in an interview on April 23, emphasized the existential risk posed to DeFi, prompting the unprecedented decision.

data.title: An account with a loss of approximately $1.5 million purchased $100,000 worth of NBA Western Conference First Round Game 6, in which the Nuggets defeated the Timberwolves.
data.description: According to Odaily Seer's monitoring, in Polymarket's prediction event "NBA Western Conference First Round Game 6 Timberwolves vs. Nuggets," an account (address: 0x26437896ed9dfeb2f69765edcafe8fdceaab39ae) that lost approximately $1.5 million purchased $100,000 worth of shares 50 minutes before the game, betting on the Nuggets defeating the Timberwolves, with an average opening price of 73¢. Game 6 of the NBA Western Conference First Round, the Timberwolves vs. Nuggets, will tip off at 9:30 AM Beijing time today. The Timberwolves currently lead the Nuggets 3-2. Timberwolves' star player Edwards is out with a knee injury, Ayo Dorsham will miss the game due to a calf injury, and DiVincenzo is out for the season with a torn right Achilles tendon. For the Nuggets, Aaron Gordon will also miss the game due to left calf muscle tension, and Payton Watson will miss the game with a right hamstring strain. Odaily Seer continuously focuses on the prediction market, seeing changes before pricing.

data.title: Significant Expiry of Cryptocurrency Options Today
data.description:
According to market reports, approximately $2.14 billion worth of cryptocurrency options are set to expire today. According to ChainCatcher, Bitcoin options have a nominal value of $1.74 billion, with a put/call ratio of 1.1 and a maximum pain point at $76,000. Ethereum options have a nominal value of $394 million, with a put/call ratio of 0.95 and a maximum pain point at $2,325.

Currently, Bitcoin's spot price is aligned with its maximum pain point, while Ethereum is trading below its respective level.

data.title: Yen Expected to Remain Weak Amid Potential Government Intervention
data.description:
The Japanese yen is anticipated to maintain its weakness even as government intervention appears likely. Wall Street Journal (Markets) posted on X, highlighting the ongoing challenges faced by the yen in the global currency market. Despite potential measures by the Japanese government to stabilize the currency, analysts suggest that the yen's depreciation may persist due to broader economic factors.

The yen has been under pressure from various economic dynamics, including interest rate differentials and global market trends. Japan's central bank has maintained a loose monetary policy, contrasting with tightening measures by other major economies, which has contributed to the yen's decline.

Market observers are closely watching for any signs of intervention by Japanese authorities, which could temporarily bolster the yen. However, the effectiveness of such actions remains uncertain, given the complex interplay of international economic forces.

The yen's performance is crucial for Japan's economy, impacting trade balances and inflation rates. As the situation unfolds, investors and policymakers are keenly monitoring developments to assess the potential implications for the global financial landscape.

data.title: Bitcoin's April Gains Face May Downside Risks
data.description: Bitcoin experienced an 11% increase throughout April. However, according to NS3.AI, analysts have expressed concerns about potential downside risks in May. They highlight historical midterm-year May reversals and on-chain resistance as factors contributing to this cautious outlook.

data.title: Bitcoin and Ethereum April Returns Show Mixed Performance
data.description:
Bitcoin's return rate reached 11.87%, marking the highest in the past 12 months, according to ChainCatcher. This figure is below the historical average return rate of 12.98% for Bitcoin in April but surpasses the historical median of 9.57% for the same month.

Ethereum's return rate for April hit 7.3%, significantly lower than its historical average return rate of 18.82% for April. However, it is above the historical median return rate of 7.3% for the month.

data.title: DeFi Experiences Record Losses Exceeding $606 Million in Under Three Weeks
data.description: Decentralized Finance (DeFi) has suffered significant setbacks, losing over $606 million across multiple protocols in less than three weeks, marking its worst month on record. According to NS3.AI, the majority of these losses, approximately 95%, were attributed to incidents involving Drift and Kelp DAO. Drift's vulnerabilities were exploited through social engineering and governance weaknesses, while Kelp DAO faced a bridge exploit that targeted its off-chain infrastructure. The breach at Kelp DAO subsequently impacted lending markets, resulting in Aave incurring an estimated $246 million in bad debt and reducing the total value locked by more than $13 billion within a span of two days.

data.title: Japan Prepared to Intervene in Currency Market Amid Speculation, Official Says
data.description: Japan's Finance Ministry official, Atsushi Mimura, stated on Friday that speculative activities in the market continue, indicating Japan's readiness to intervene to support the yen. According to Jin10, when asked about potential intervention by Tokyo in the currency market, Mimura said, "I do not intend to comment on the actions we will take next. But I want to tell you that Japan's Golden Week holiday has just begun." Mimura declined to comment on whether Japan intervened in the foreign exchange market on Thursday. When questioned about whether exchange rate fluctuations are still considered speculative, Mimura responded, "My view has not changed." Analysts note that due to the Golden Week holiday, Japan's stock market will be closed from Monday to Wednesday next week. This could lead to significant yen volatility due to insufficient market liquidity.

data.title: Stablecoin Payments Could Transform Financial Infrastructure, Says Stanford Professor
data.description:
Stanford Professor Ruizhe Jia highlighted the potential impact of stablecoin-based payments during the EASYResidency workshop. YZi Labs posted on X. Jia emphasized that stablecoins could significantly alter financial infrastructure, particularly in fragmented markets. He noted that the competition to dominate the payment sector is intensifying, with the development of innovative applications being crucial.

Jia pointed to Alipay and WeChat Pay as examples of how controlling the payment layer can lead to becoming a central hub for various financial services, including savings, lending, and investing. He suggested that stablecoins might represent the next evolution of this model in many regions, although the market is still in its early stages.

data.title: AI TRENDS | Demis Hassabis Discusses AGI Timeline and Entrepreneurial Advice
data.description: Demis Hassabis, a prominent figure in artificial intelligence, shared insights on the timeline for achieving Artificial General Intelligence (AGI), suggesting that it could be realized by 2030. According to PANews, Hassabis emphasized the importance for deep tech entrepreneurs to prepare in advance for this significant development. He also reflected on the potential for scientific breakthroughs following AlphaFold and addressed challenges related to reasoning flaws and memory limitations. The podcast encapsulates these key points, offering valuable advice for those in the field.

data.title: X Platform Introduces Cashtags Feature for Real-Time Asset Tracking
data.description: X Platform has launched the Cashtags feature on its web interface, offering real-time charts and posts for each asset, positioning itself as a key component of user trading terminals. According to PANews, this follows the earlier introduction of the Cashtags feature on iPhone devices in the United States and Canada, providing users with real-time financial data.

data.title: Wasabi Protocol Confirms Solana Contracts Unaffected by Recent Vulnerability
data.description: Wasabi Protocol has announced that its contracts on the Solana blockchain remain secure and are not impacted by the recent vulnerability affecting its EVM deployment. According to ChainCatcher, the issue is confined to Wasabi's Ethereum Virtual Machine (EVM) deployment. The team is collaborating with top security experts and has reached out to law enforcement agencies, including the FBI. Further updates will be provided as they become available.

data.title: Tesla Reports Over $500 Million Revenue from SpaceX and xAI Sales
data.description: Tesla updated its report on Thursday, revealing that it generated over $500 million in revenue through product sales to SpaceX and xAI. According to Jin10, Tesla had previously disclosed in January that its business dealings with xAI amounted to approximately $430.1 million. The updated report on Thursday indicates that Tesla earned an additional $143.3 million from SpaceX.

data.title: India Faces Intense Heatwave Amid Energy Shortages
data.description: India is expected to experience a severe heatwave until the monsoon rains arrive in June, Bloomberg posted on X. The country is already facing higher-than-average temperatures, which are putting additional pressure on power grids during a period of energy shortages. The situation is exacerbated by the increased demand for electricity as people seek relief from the heat. Authorities are closely monitoring the situation and are urging citizens to conserve energy wherever possible. The upcoming monsoon season is anticipated to bring some relief, but until then, the nation must contend with the challenges posed by the extreme weather conditions.
